arxiv_constraints_applied: PDFLaTeX-compatible source with the upload-ready preview selecting pdflatex

section_scores.rationale: Improved title hierarchy, compact author block, abstract emphasis, PDF metadata, and readable 11pt page geometry.
Clear opening hierarchy with improved line breaking, microtypography, link colour discipline, and stable paragraph spacing.
Evidence tables, mapping flow, network summary, and actual graph visualisations now create a strong visual evidence path.
Readable long-form discussion with improved section separation and no observed overflow in the generated PDF.
Dense methodological subsections remain readable with strengthened subsection hierarchy and arXiv-safe URL wrapping.
Concise section benefits from clearer section rule and consistent text measure.
Meets target with good readability; could improve further only with a short limitations table, which would add length.
URL breaking and coloured links improve navigation while keeping arXiv-safe relative source packaging.
Plain but professional. The section should remain conservative because declaration text should not be over-designed.
TikZ summaries plus three actual graph renders provide the strongest visual presentation surface in the paper.
Manual bibliography is stable and source-checked; visual polish is intentionally conventional for arXiv reliability.
Dedicated page, anchored terms, and improved typography make the glossary usable as a navigational aid.
Dedicated page and hyperlink anchors support reader navigation and machine-readable terminology expansion.

residual_risks: arXiv-rendered PDF still requires final human inspection after upload because arXiv's Submission 1.5 processor is authoritative.
Further visual styling should avoid custom fonts, shell escape, and fragile packages that would reduce arXiv reliability.
A journal-specific class may later change the scoring baseline and require a separate scorecard.
